parsed_review.summary: CP00-933 is a coherent descriptor-only HRX bridge pack (AIReviewQueue tail rows RP30.P04.M09.S04-S08 + AdminPolicy foundation rows RP30.P04.M10.S01-S05; 10 units; risk A), and it is the legitimate live-next pack, monotonic after CP00-932 per docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json. The pack-level claim is production_ready=false / runtime_ready=false with flag hrx_ai_review_admin_policy_tail_bridge_descriptor_verified, and that descriptor-only claim is fully supported by read-only inspection: HRX_CP933_PACK_BINDING, HRX_CP933_REQUIRED_SECTION_ROWS, the descriptor factory (registry.js:8016), row generation (registry.js:2756), the coverage/descriptor/contract validators (validators.js:6310/6349/6465), the regenerated contract projection (contracts/hrx-people-contract.json cp933/latest_projection/no_write_attestation), and three model tests (model.test.js:1883-1961) all agree. Every runtime/record-write/review-action/rule-execution/final-judgment/finalization flag, plus real employee/candidate/payroll/document data and credential/secret inclusion, is held false; no real data or secrets leak in source. Hermes packets are deterministic-only with emits_runtime_receipt=false and the Claude packet is read_only with promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false; authority_boundary keeps claude_is_final_approval=false and human approval required for runtime opening at CP00-934. Manifest, command-evidence, construction-inspection, and adjudication consistently report production_ready=false and a pending-review status with a null receipt (correct, since this review produces the receipt) — no overclaim of runtime or enterprise trust. The readiness auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1432-byte valid JSON), and the unrelated dirty worktree files are correctly excluded from pack scope. One non-blocking P3 provenance note: tests/validators were verified by reading source and command-evidence reports exit 0 for a subset only; the full validation ladder is marked pending_final_validation and must complete green before status flips to closed. No P0/P1/P2 issues.

findings.title: Full validation ladder pending; verified by read-only inspection, not execution
findings.details: command-evidence records exit_code 0 only for node --check syntax checks, node --test packages/hrx/test/model.test.js, and npm run rp30:hrx:validate; the broader closeout ladder (npm test, npm run validate, npm run spec:requirements:validate, npm run weighted:validate, npm run fullplan:validate, npm run goal:closeout:validate, npm run rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) is marked gate_outcome=pending_final_validation with hermes_gate.status=pending. Under read-only constraints I confirmed the CP933 binding (registry.js:695), descriptor factory (registry.js:8016), validators (validators.js:6310/6349/6465), three model tests (model.test.js:1883-1961), and the regenerated contract projection (contracts/hrx-people-contract.json:674,66653,68947) are internally consistent and descriptor-only, but I did not execute these commands. This is expected review-before-ladder sequencing, not a code defect.
findings.recommendation: Before flipping manifest/construction-inspection/adjudication status from in_progress/pending to closed and writing the review receipt, run the full validation ladder to green and confirm git diff --check is clean. Treat as a closeout-orchestrator confirmation step, not a code change.
findings.post_review_disposition: Informational provenance note; non-blocking. Closeout orchestrator to confirm the validation ladder is green and the worktree clean before status flip and receipt write.
